question: The Oberoi family is part of a hotel company that has a head office in what city?

no_tool_rollouts.text:
The Oberoi family is part of the InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG), which has its head office in Cambridge, United Kingdom. Therefore, the answer is:

<answer>Cambridge</answer>

To determine the city where the head office of the Oberoi hotel company is located, we need to consider the history and current status of the Oberoi group. The Oberoi family is well-known for founding several luxury hotels in India and other countries. Given this context, the head office of the Oberoi group is typically located in India.

The headquarters of the Oberoi Group of Hotels, which includes prominent hotels like the Oberoi Suites in New Delhi, is in New Delhi, India.

<answer>New Delhi</answer>

To determine the city where the head office of the Oberoi hotel company is located, we need to consider that the Oberoi family is known for having a significant role in the hospitality industry, particularly in India. The Oberoi Group is one of the leading hotel chains in India and has its headquarters in New Delhi.

Therefore, the head office of the Oberoi hotel company is in New Delhi.

<answer>New Delhi</answer>

The Oberoi family is part of the InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG). IHG's head office is located in Des Moines, Iowa, USA. Therefore, the answer is:

<answer>Des Moines</answer>

results = search("Oberoi family hotel company head office")

[1] 
"The Oberoi Group"
The Oberoi Group The Oberoi Group is a hotel group with its head office in Delhi. Founded in 1934, the company owns and/or operates 35 luxury hotels and two river cruise ships in six countries, primarily under its Oberoi Hotels & Resorts and Trident Hotels brands. The foundations of the Oberoi Group date back to 1934 when Rai Bahadur Mohan Singh Oberoi, the founder of the group, bought two properties: the Maidens in Delhi and the Clarke's in Shimla. In the following years Oberoi, assisted by his two sons, Tilak Raj Singh Oberoi and Prithvi Raj Singh Oberoi (P.R.S. Oberoi),

[2] 
"Mohan Singh Oberoi"
of Ludwig Mittel Huber. They had a son Arjun Singh Oberoi. Oberoi nephew Brij Raj Oberoi also called as ""Diamond Oberoi""(nickname given by M.S Oberoi) consciously followed M.S Oberoi footsteps and contnuied on the family business, by operating several Heritage hotels in the Himalayas. Many Other relatives Mohan Oberoi, have followed his footsteps, and have worked in the hospitality industry, notably for the Oberoi group. Mohan Singh Oberoi Rai Bahadur Mohan Singh Oberoi (15 August 1898 – 3 May 2002) was an Indian hotelier, the founder and chairman of Oberoi Hotels & Resorts, India's second-largest hotel company, with 35 hotels

[3] 
"Prithvi Raj Singh Oberoi"
at the Oberoi Farms farmhouse -cum-office on the outskirts of Delhi. He has a nephew Arjun Oberoi. He currently looks after the Project Development and designated as Managing Director- Development. Prithvi Raj Singh Oberoi Prithviraj Singh ""Biki"" Oberoi (born 1929) is the Executive Chairman of the EIH Limited (formerly East India Hotels), , the flagship company of India’s The Oberoi Group, which runs a chain of luxury hotels, The Trident hotels and Oberoi Hotels & Resorts., the third largest hospitality chain in India. In 2008, the Government of India awarded Oberoi with the Padma Vibushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, in
<context>The head office of The Oberoi Group is in Delhi.</context><answer>Delhi</answer>

Founded in 1934, the company owns and/or operates 35 luxury hotels and two river cruise ships in six countries, primarily under its Oberoi Hotels & Resorts and Trident Hotels brands. The foundations of the Oberoi Group date back to 1934 when Rai Bahadur Mohan Singh Oberoi, the founder of the group, bought two properties: the Maidens in Delhi and the Clarke's in Shimla. In the following years Oberoi, assisted by his two sons, Tilak Raj Singh Oberoi and Prithvi Raj Singh Oberoi (P.R.S. Oberoi),

[2] 
"Mohan Singh Oberoi"
of Ludwig Mittel Huber. They had a son Arjun Singh Oberoi. Oberoi nephew Brij Raj Oberoi also called as ""Diamond Oberoi""(nickname given by M.S Oberoi) consciously followed M.S Oberoi footsteps and contnuied on the family business, by operating several Heritage hotels in the Himalayas. Many Other relatives Mohan Oberoi, have followed his footsteps, and have worked in the hospitality industry, notably for the Oberoi group. Mohan Singh Oberoi Rai Bahadur Mohan Singh Oberoi (15 August 1898 – 3 May 2002) was an Indian hotelier, the founder and chairman of Oberoi Hotels & Resorts, India's second-largest hotel company, with 35 hotels

[3] 
"Prithvi Raj Singh Oberoi"
at the Oberoi Farms farmhouse -cum-office on the outskirts of Delhi. He has a nephew Arjun Oberoi. He currently looks after the Project Development and designated as Managing Director- Development. Prithvi Raj Singh Oberoi Prithviraj Singh ""Biki"" Oberoi (born 1929) is the Executive Chairman of the EIH Limited (formerly East India Hotels), , the flagship company of India’s The Oberoi Group, which runs a chain of luxury hotels, The Trident hotels and Oberoi Hotels & Resorts., the third largest hospitality chain in India. In 2008, the Government of India awarded Oberoi with the Padma Vibushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, in
